directive_executed.title: Investigate curmudgeon c-cycle carry-over re-verification hygiene

tasks_completed.summary: Surveyed 576 curmudgeon review files across 82 multi-cycle targets. 314 cN→cN+1 transitions; 42 (13.4%) contain carry-over markers; 33 (10.5%) reference at least one ISS closed before review time. After filtering for curmudgeon-acknowledged-closure dispositions, 4-29 candidates remain depending on filter strictness. Estimated TRUE stale-without-acknowledgement frequency: 3-5% of multi-cycle transitions (lower bound from ISS-traceable 1.3% + text-only carryovers that elude ISS-name heuristics). Concrete instance from directive (WIN-055.c4) VERIFIED: current data/wins.json:WIN-055.sub_rationale contains no 'See Part 6.13' phrase; ISS-2025 closed 29.5h before c4 review removed that cross-reference.

Three options considered (A: ISS-closed list check, B: drift-audit fingerprint + per-hole text re-grep, C: decider-side intake filter). Selected B with explicit rejection rationale for A (false-negative blind spot on text-only carryovers — demonstrably misses WIN-055.c4 case) and C (catches cost too late, after analyst-Opus spent). Implementation patch specified: ~18 lines added to curmudgeon.md Step 5, no schema changes, no consumer-side changes.

Four counter-arguments addressed: (1) downstream OBE-dismissal handles it — partly true but loses analyst-Opus cycles; (2) over-suppression of legitimate carry-overs — mechanism rewrites rather than suppresses, preserves audit trail; (3) prompt addition creep — 4% growth, paired with offsetting Mode-3 reductions; (4) post-write observability sufficient — fragile (analyst-Mode-1 catch was incidental, summary-overwrite race forced operator-via-cowork-Claude filing).

directive_executed.directive_status_after_run: completed (additive-edit per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE exception: status='completed', completed_at, completed_by_run, prop_id_authored)

findings.summary: DIRECTIVE-20260518-001 executed. PROP-047 filed with status=design-pending-operator-review. Directive marked completed.

Root disk df=93%, 722MB free. Project footprint=736MB (warning threshold). Recurring symptom: analyst run 16:49Z reported 'disk 100% on /sessions' and skipped clone refresh, running FUSE-only. No /tmp/*-clone directories present to clean (auto-empowerment finds nothing). The 736MB is dominated by FUSE workspace baseline (~732MB). If analyst clone-refresh failures persist, operator may need to investigate sandbox disk-image creep, or whether some other agent (decider, curmudgeon) is filling /tmp transiently within the same hour.

evidence_files: monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt (line: 'Step 0a clone refresh skipped — no existing clone, and full clone attempt failed (disk 100% on /sessions). FUSE-only run.')

findings.summary: Curmudgeon c-cycle stale-carry-over pattern documented in PROP-047. 1.3% iss-traceable + estimated 2-4% text-only = ~3-5% of multi-cycle transitions. Most carry-overs are legitimate (curmudgeon explicitly verifies defect text remains despite ISS patch); the failure mode is the narrow case of c-N+1 author copying c-N text without re-grepping. PROP-047 proposes a per-hole grep mandate inside curmudgeon Step 5.
